title: cymopterus grayanus var. filicinus
intro: Cymopterus grayanus var. filicinus, also known as mountain parsley, is a perennial herb of the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the western United States. Its leaves and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Cymopterus grayanus var. filicinus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
